messages: When should I step away from a single stuck problem versus continuing to grind it out? Any simple decision rules?
How can I detect diminishing returns when increasing the number of full practice exams so I don't waste time?
Are study playlists with binaural beats worth experimenting with, or do they tend to be placebo for most people?
What's a practical approach to interleaving topics when many concepts overlap and I have limited time?
Would running a short peer-led workshop once a week be more effective than solo practice for deeply understanding a topic?
Quick mnemonic techniques for ordered multi-step problem solving—what actually works in practice?
Does maintaining an 'error log' of every mistake help long-term, or can it become demoralizing if not managed well?
After a disappointing practice test, how should I plan the next day so the work is corrective rather than punitive?
If I plan to arrive 45 minutes before the scheduled start to allow for check-in, and my usual commute takes 25 minutes, what departure time should I set?
Considering the total time available and the combination of quick and longer problems, how should I divide time per quick problem if I want to reserve a block at the end for the longer ones?
Given the strict material restrictions they enforce, which formulas, constants, or worked-example templates should I prioritize fitting onto the single physical reference I'm permitted to bring?
Because my seat will be assigned and check-in matches registration to ID, would you recommend bringing any extra documents or backups to avoid delays on arrival?

With my limited weekday availability and the session rhythm I prefer, how many focused sessions are reasonable per week, and how would you balance core theory review versus active practice across them?

If I run one full dress rehearsal under authentic constraints—same time pressure and the same limitations on reference material and calculator—how should I structure that practice day to get the most realistic feedback?
The registration barcode on my paperwork is how they verify me. If that barcode or the one on my ID fails to scan at the center, what pragmatic steps should I take to expedite verification?
Finally, assuming I follow your recommended pacing, what's a sensible, low-stress plan for the final 48 hours before the day so I stay sharp without burning out?
